# Database Schema Reference
# Generated by GSSDBAGENT on 2026-03-22 14:38 UTC

> **When to use this file:** Activity = Developing, Prototyping (database queries, schema lookups)

## Database Overview
- **Engine**: Pervasive.SQL 14.21.0002 000 (GLOBALPLA)
- **Tables**: 1977 | **Views**: 1304
- **Total Columns**: 48,098

## SQL Dialect (Actian Zen -- CRITICAL)

> For the full Zen SQL language reference (data types, DDL, DML, scalar functions, procedures, performance), see **`agents/AGENTS.ZEN.md`**. The rules below are GSS-specific conventions layered on top of the Zen dialect.

- String functions: Use RTRIM() / LTRIM(), NOT TRIM()
- Row limiting: SELECT TOP N * FROM table (Zen also supports LIMIT/OFFSET, but use TOP for GSS convention and compatibility with older engine versions; see `agents/AGENTS.ZEN.md` for full syntax)
- String columns are space-padded to fixed widths -- ALWAYS RTRIM query results
- System table columns with $ in names cannot be used in SQL WHERE/SELECT -- query all rows and filter in code
- Parameterized queries use ? placeholder (not @param or :param)
- No @@SERVERNAME or similar SQL Server conventions
- SELECT COUNT(*) works on both tables and views
- Views are fully queryable (SELECT, WHERE, JOIN, etc.)

## CRITICAL! Views-First for READ Operations
- When performing ANY read operation (SELECT, COUNT, EXISTS, JOINs for data retrieval), ALWAYS use the corresponding `V_` view instead of the base table if one exists.
- The view name is deterministic: `V_` + base table name (e.g., `ORDER_HEADER` -> `V_ORDER_HEADER`, `AP_CHECKS` -> `V_AP_CHECKS`).
- Check the table's schema group file (`schema/*.md`) to confirm whether a view exists before writing a query. Many groups have views for most or all tables (see the Table Groups section for view counts per group).
- Tables with NO corresponding view (e.g., ATG, EVENT, GUI, KB, MENU, MISC, NTP, OPP, PDT, PY, RVZ, SUBOPP, TMS, VMS, and most Z_ report tables) should be queried directly.
- WRITE operations (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) should continue to target the base tables directly -- views are read-only.
- This rule applies to all query construction: ad-hoc queries, application code, reports, and any SQL generation.

## Naming Conventions

### Tables
- SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE with underscores
- Common prefixes indicate module: AP, APS, APSV3, AR, ATG, BI, BILL, BOL, BOM, CASH, CC, CFG, CNCA, COMMENTS, COMMISSION, COMPANY, CON, CONFIG, CONSIGN, CRM, CUST, CUSTOM, CUSTOMER, DTL, ECN, EDI, EMP, EMPL, EMPLOYEE, ENG, EVENT, FIN, FORECAST, FREIGHT, GL, GUI, HOOK, INV, INVC, INVENTORY, ITEM, JOB, JOBS, KB, LASER, LINK, LOT, LPART, MAT, MATL, MENU, MISC, NTP, OE, OPP, ORD, ORDER, PART, PAY, PAYR, PAYROLL, PDT, PERF, PL, PO, POHIST, PREV, PRICE, PROJ, PROJECT, PROSPECT, PRT, PURCH, PY, PYRL, QTE, QUAL, QUALITY, QUOTE, RECEIVER, REQSN, RMA, ROUTER, RTR, RVZ, SA, SALES, SCHED, SHIP, SHIPMENT, SHIPPING, SO, STAGING, SUBOPP, TASK, TAX, TIME, TMS, USER, USRSCR, VAP, VAT, VEND, VENDOR, VMS, W2, WC, WIP, WIRELESS, WO, Y, Z
- _ACCT suffix = account linkage
- _DETAIL suffix = detail/line item record
- _DTL suffix = detail/line item record
- _HDR suffix = header/master record
- _HEADER suffix = header/master record
- _INV suffix = invoice-related
- _REFACE suffix = reface/revision
- Z_, Y_, BI_, PRT_, LASER_ prefixes = report tables
### Views
- ALL views prefixed with V_ followed by the base table name
- Views are fully queryable (SELECT, COUNT, etc.)

## Cross-Module Join Paths (Bridge Tables)

These are the critical join paths that span module boundaries. Bridge tables connect two major entities that belong to different modules. Agents writing queries that cross module lines should use these paths.

### Sales -> Manufacturing
- **ORDER_LINES** -> **ORDER_TO_WO** (`ORDER_NO = ORDER_NO`, `RECORD_NO = ORDER_LINE`) -> **JOB_HEADER** (`JOB = JOB`, `SUFFIX = SUFFIX`)
- **ORDER_LINES** -> **JOB_HEADER** (`JOB = JOB`, `SUFFIX = SUFFIX`) — direct link when order line has a job
- **PO_HEADER** -> **PO_WO_XREF** (`PURCHASE_ORDER = PO`) -> **JOB_HEADER** (`JOB = WO`, `SUFFIX = WO_SUFF`)

### Sales -> Shipping
- **ORDER_HEADER** -> **SHIPMENT_HEADER** (`ORDER_NO = ORDER_NO`, `ORDER_SUFFIX = ORDER_SUFFIX`)
- **ORDER_HEADER** -> **OE_MULTI_SHIP** (`ORDER_NO -> SHIP_SEQ`) — ship-to addresses
- **ORDER_LINES** -> **SHIPMENT_LINES** (via ORDER_NO + ORDER_SUFFIX + RECORD_NO)

### Sales -> Invoicing/History
- **ORDER_HEADER** -> **ORDER_HIST_HEAD** (`ORDER_NO = ORDER_NO`) — after invoice
- **SHIPMENT_HEADER** -> **ORDER_HIST_HEAD** (`ORDER_SUFFIX = ORIG_SHPMT_SEQ`)

### Manufacturing -> Inventory
- **JOB_HEADER** -> **INVENTORY_MSTR** (`PART = PART`, `LOCATION = LOCATION`)
- **JOB_OPERATIONS** -> **WORKCENTERS** (`MACHINE = MACHINE`)
- **ROUTER_LINE** -> **INVENTORY_MSTR** (`PART = PART`)

### Purchasing -> Inventory
- **PO_LINES** -> **INVENTORY_MSTR** (`PART = PART`, `LOCATION = LOCATION`)
- **PO_RECEIVER** -> **INVENTORY_MSTR** (`PART = PART`)

### Customer / Vendor Shared References
- `*.CUSTOMER -> CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUSTOMER`
- `*.VENDOR -> VENDOR_MASTER.VENDOR`
- `*.GL_ACCOUNT -> GL_MASTER.GL_ACCOUNT`
- `*.LAST_CHG_BY -> USER_INFORMATION.GS_USER`

## Foreign Key Relationships

### Named Pervasive Constraints

These are formal FK constraints defined in the Pervasive/Actian Zen database engine:

- API_RESPONSE -> API_REQUEST (FK: FK_API_RESPONSE_REQUEST_ID)
- BILLS -> CATEGORIES (FK: FK_1CAT_ID)
- NTP_QUOTE_DUPLICATE_CHECK -> NTP_QUOTE_HEADER (FK: FK_8QUOTE_ID)
- NTP_QUOTE_FILM_LINE -> NTP_QUOTE_HEADER (FK: FK_4QUOTE_ID)
- NTP_QUOTE_FILM_LINE -> NTP_FILM_LINE_MASTER (FK: FK_1FILM_LINE_ID)
- NTP_QUOTE_HISTORY_INDEX -> NTP_QUOTE_HEADER (FK: FK_7QUOTE_ID)
- NTP_QUOTE_LAYER -> NTP_QUOTE_HEADER (FK: FK_1QUOTE_ID)
- NTP_QUOTE_LAYER_RESIN -> NTP_QUOTE_LAYER (FK: FK_3QUOTE_ID)
- NTP_QUOTE_LAYER_RESIN -> NTP_RESIN_MASTER (FK: FK_2RESIN_ID)
- NTP_QUOTE_OPTIONS -> NTP_QUOTE_HEADER (FK: FK_5QUOTE_ID)
- NTP_QUOTE_OPTIONS -> NTP_OPTION_MASTER (FK: FK_2OPTION_ID)
- NTP_QUOTE_PACKAGING -> NTP_QUOTE_HEADER (FK: FK_9QUOTE_ID)
- NTP_QUOTE_PACKAGING -> NTP_PACKAGING_MASTER (FK: FK_2PACKAGING_ID)
- NTP_QUOTE_PRICE_BREAK -> NTP_QUOTE_HEADER (FK: FK_6QUOTE_ID)
- NTP_QUOTE_PRICING -> NTP_QUOTE_HEADER (FK: FK_2QUOTE_ID)
- NTP_QUOTE_PRODUCT_SPECS -> NTP_QUOTE_HEADER (FK: FK_QUOTE_ID)
- PART_TOL_MAP -> TOLERANCE_MATRIX (FK: FK_PART_TOL_TOLCODE)
- PART_TOLERANCE -> TOLERANCE_MATRIX (FK: FK_1TOLCODE)
- PP_INCOME -> PAY_PERIODS (FK: FK_2PP_ID)
- PP_ITEMS -> PAY_PERIODS (FK: FK_1PP_ID)
- PP_ITEMS -> BILLS (FK: FK_2BILL_ID)

### Application-Layer FK Relationships (5,040 edges across 98 modules)

The application layer defines 5,040 column-level FK relationships spanning 3,637 unique table pairs. Full details are embedded in the individual `schema/*.md` files under the `## Relationships` section for each group.

Summary by module (unique table-pair count):

| Module | FK Pairs | Module | FK Pairs | Module | FK Pairs |
|--------|----------|--------|----------|--------|----------|
| AP | 154 | GL | 167 | ORDER | 253 |
| AR | 69 | INV | 90 | PO | 182 |
| ATG | 45 | INVENTORY | 43 | QUOTE | 83 |
| BOL | 51 | ITEM | 25 | RMA | 46 |
| BOM | 27 | JOB | 128 | RTR | 28 |
| CFG | 22 | JOBS | 16 | SA | 71 |
| CNCA | 21 | LASER | 19 | SHIPMENT | 125 |
| COMMISSION | 11 | LOT | 24 | SHIP | 38 |
| CONFIG | 32 | MAT | 8 | STAGING | 16 |
| CONSIGN | 59 | MATL | 12 | TAX | 12 |
| CRM | 99 | MENU | 30 | USER | 18 |
| CUST | 52 | OE | 89 | VAP | 9 |
| CUSTOMER | 22 | ORD | 51 | VAT | 23 |
| DTL | 58 | PART | 11 | VEND | 18 |
| ECN | 25 | PAY | 24 | VENDOR | 26 |
| EDI | 45 | PAYROLL | 55 | WC | 8 |
| EMP | 12 | FORECAST | 61 | WIP | 15 |
| EMPLOYEE | 20 | POHIST | 18 | WIRELESS | 27 |
| FREIGHT | 13 | PRICE | 9 | WO | 8 |
| PROJECT | 38 | PROSPECT | 22 | MISC | 517 |

Common FK patterns across the database:
- `*.LAST_CHG_BY -> USER_INFORMATION.GS_USER` (audit trail on most tables)
- `*.GL_ACCOUNT -> GL_MASTER.GL_ACCOUNT` (GL account linkage)
- `*.TAX_AUTH -> AR_TAX_TABLES.AUTH` (tax authority references)
- `*.PART_NUMBER -> INVENTORY_MSTR.PART_NUMBER` (inventory references)
- `*.CUSTOMER -> CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUSTOMER` (customer references)
- `*.VENDOR -> VENDOR_MASTER.VENDOR` (vendor references)
- `*.JOB -> JOB_HEADER.JOB` (job references)
- `*.BRANCH -> ACCTG_BRANCH.BRANCH` (branch references)
- `*.UM -> UM_CODES.UM` (unit of measure references)
- `*.CURRENCY -> EXCHANGE_CURRENCY.CURRENCY` (multi-currency references)

## View Cross-Reference

All 1,304 views follow the pattern `V_` + base table name (e.g., `V_AP_CHECKS` -> `AP_CHECKS`).
To look up a view's columns and conversion details, find the base table in the corresponding `schema/*.md` file — each table section now includes a `### View:` subsection documenting which columns are transformed.

### Why Views Are Better for Reads

Views provide **typed date/time columns** instead of the raw fixed-width strings stored in base tables. This means date comparisons, sorting, and arithmetic work natively in SQL without manual parsing. The base tables store dates as space-padded CHAR strings; the views convert them to `SQL_DATE` and `SQL_TIME` types.

### View Column Conversion Patterns

All 1,304 views are single-table SELECTs (no JOINs, no WHERE clauses, no aggregation). They apply up to three conversion patterns:

| Pattern | Source Format | Output Type | Validation | Default on Invalid |
|---------|-------------|-------------|------------|-------------------|
| YYYYMMDD -> SQL_DATE | 8-char string with century (e.g., "20240606") | SQL_DATE | Year 1900-2999, Month 01-12, Day 01-31 | 1900-01-01 |
| MMDDYY -> SQL_DATE | 6-char legacy string (e.g., "060624") | SQL_DATE | Year < 80 = 20xx, Year >= 80 = 19xx; Month 01-12, Day 01-31 | 1900-01-01 |
| HHMMSS -> SQL_TIME | 6-char time string (e.g., "143052") | SQL_TIME | Hour <= 24, Minute < 60, Second < 60 | 00:00:00 |

### View Statistics

- Views with YYYYMMDD date conversions: 470
- Views with MMDDYY date conversions: 168
- Views with HHMMSS time conversions: 309
- Views with computed columns: 26
- Pure passthrough views (no transformations): 717
- Multi-view tables (e.g., V_TABLE, V_TABLE2, V_TABLE3): 20 base tables have multiple views projecting different column subsets

### Notes on Special Views

- ~5 view SQL files are truncated/broken (prefixed with `-- WARNING: Could not query view schema`): V_BI_WOROUTER, V_CON_KPI_CALENDAR, V_PAYR_MAN_TC_DEDCT, V_PAYR_MAN_TC_EMPLR, V_PAYR_MAN_TC_RATES
- Some views have `/* computed */` annotations on columns that are derived at the table engine level
- The COMMENTS table group has views that alias a base table named "A" -- this is a quirk of the comments system
